Farn PolrrrcnI- PnecrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 9581+2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
August 5,2004
Jondi Gumz
Post Office Box 638
Santa Cruz, CA 95061
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-04-174
Dear Ms. Gumz:
This letter is in response to your request for written advice on the provisions of the
Political Reform Act.' The Fair Political Practices Commission provides formal written advice
to any otgarization/person or their authorized representafive whose duties are in question under
the Political Reform Act. (Government Code Section 83114(b).) Under the provisions of
regulation 18329 (bX2XA), certain information must be provided:
"(2) Requests for formal written adyice will not be acted upon unless the
following requirernents are met:
(A) The narne, title or position, and mailing address of the person whose duties
are in question are provided. In addition, if the request is submitted by an
authorized representative, it shall contain a specific statement that sucL
authorization has been made."
If you believe a violation of the Political Reform Act has occurred, you may file a
complaint with our agency (form enclosed).
Should you have additional questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to
contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
t
ohn W. Wallace
General Counsel
Enclosure
Jwwjg
By:

1899'/, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: Ms. Gumz submitted a request for formal written advice regarding the Political Reform Act, but the request does not meet the statutory requirements for formal written advice.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide formal written advice because the request does not comply with regulation 18329(b)(2)(A), which requires that the name, title or position, and mailing address of the person whose duties are in question be provided, and if submitted by an authorized representative, must include specific authorization documentation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter rather than a substantive advice letter. No traditional question or conclusion sections are present. The letter explains why the request cannot be processed and references the regulatory requirements that were not met.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Ms. Gumz submitted a request for formal written advice regarding the Political Reform Act, but the request does not meet the statutory requirements for formal written advice.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC cannot provide formal written advice because the request does not comply with regulation 18329(b)(2)(A), which requires that the name, title or position, and mailing address of the person whose duties are in question be provided, and if submitted by an authorized representative, must include specific authorization documentation.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide formal written advice to Jondi Gumz regarding the Political Reform Act because the request does not meet the minimum statutory requirements under regulation 18329(b)(2)(A). The FPPC advises that if a violation has occurred, a complaint may be filed with the agency.
